title: Lipidomics identifications and quantitation
author: Jennifer Kyle
materials: Select raw files to be processed.
Double click on “alignmentresults…” (red arrow, lower left).
Choose the export directory by clicking on “Browse”.
Choose the export directory by clicking on "Browse".

steps.title: Open "MSDIAL
steps.instruction: Open "MSDIAL.exe".

steps.title: Under “Data type (MS/MS), check “Centroid data” (top red arrow)
steps.instruction: Under “Data type (MS/MS), check “Centroid data” (top red arrow).

steps.title: Under “Ion mode” select the ionization...
steps.instruction: Under “Ion mode” select the ionization mode that aligns with the files to be processed (e.g., positive or negative ion mode)(red rectangle).

steps.title: Click “Next”
steps.instruction: Click “Next”.

steps.title: Click “Browse"
steps.instruction: Click “Browse".

steps.title: Click "Next"
steps.instruction: Click "Next".

steps.title: Select the following parameters per tab...
steps.instruction: Select the following parameters per tab as shown (the parameters can also be uploaded using the “parameters_POS.med” file).

steps.title: Optional – fill out remaining boxes...
steps.instruction: Optional – fill out remaining boxes with type of samples, batch, analytical order, etc.

steps.title: Click "Browse" Beside the "Project file...
steps.instruction: Click "Browse" Beside the "Project file path:" field (red circle below).

steps.title: "Analysis parameter setting" tab
steps.instruction: "Analysis parameter setting" tab. For “MSP file:” click “select” and check the following lipid subclasses for the " positive ionization ". Note: above are the subclasses selected for POS ionization. NEG ionization selections used will be shown at the end of the document (post data export).

steps.title: "Adduct" tab. Note
steps.instruction: "Adduct" tab. Note: See the end of the document for NEG mode selection

steps.title: "Alignment" tab. Note
steps.instruction: "Alignment" tab. Note: see end of document for NEG mode selection

steps.title: "Alignment" tab
steps.instruction: "Alignment" tab. Select “Together with Alignment” and click “Finished.

steps.title: Click “EIC aligned spot” to view aligned peaks
steps.instruction: Click “EIC aligned spot” to view aligned peaks.

steps.title: Click “Show ion table” (red rectangle)
steps.instruction: Click “Show ion table” (red rectangle).

steps.title: Once the alignment table opens (red...
steps.instruction: Once the alignment table opens (red box, below), it is recommended to sort by “Metabolite name” column then “RT(min)” column.

steps.title: Manually validate the identifications by examining the MS/MS spectra
steps.instruction: Manually validate the identifications by examining the MS/MS spectra.

steps.title: Parameter selection shown above is the...
steps.instruction: Parameter selection shown above is the same for both ionization modes with the following exceptions.

steps.title: Click the “Export” tab
steps.instruction: Click the “Export” tab.

steps.title: To examine chromatograms to adjust and/or...
steps.instruction: To examine chromatograms to adjust and/or validate integration/selection, right-click by chromatograms and choose “Table viewer for curating each chromatogram” and/or “Aligned chromatogram viewer for simultaneous curations”.

steps.title: Check "Raw data matrix (Height)
steps.instruction: Check "Raw data matrix (Height).

steps.title: Click "Export"
steps.instruction: Click "Export".

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: this protocol is used on the samples generated using the protocol titled : " Protocol for the LC-MS/MS-based lipidomics assay of samples generated using the MPLEx method "
